2605.09765.title: WISTERIA: Learning Clinical Representations from Noisy Supervision via Multi-View Consistency in Electronic Health Records
2605.09765.paper_summary: Representation learning in electronic health records (EHR) has largely followed paradigms inherited from natural language processing, relying on sequence modeling and reconstruction based objectives that treat clinical labels as ground truth. However, real world clinical supervision is inherently weak, arising from heterogeneous, noisy, and institution specific labeling processes such as billing codes, heuristic phenotypes, and incomplete annotations. In this work, we propose WISTERIA, a weakly supervised representation learning framework that models labels as stochastic observations of an underlying latent clinical state. Instead of optimizing against a single supervision signal, WISTERIA constructs multiple weak supervision operators and learns representations by enforcing consistency across their induced label distributions. This multi view formulation induces an implicit denoising mechanism, allowing the model to recover clinically meaningful structure by reconciling disagreement between noisy labelers. We further incorporate ontology aware regularization in the label space to impose semantic structure over supervision signals. Empirically, WISTERIA improves predictive performance across standard EHR benchmarks, demonstrates strong robustness to label noise, and exhibits superior cross institutional generalization compared to sequence based pretraining objectives. These results suggest that explicitly modeling the supervision process rather than treating labels as fixed targets provides a more appropriate inductive bias for learning robust and clinically meaningful representations from EHR data.
2605.09765.paper_summary_zh: 電子健康紀錄（EHR）中的表徵學習在很大程度上遵循了從自然語言處理繼承的範式，依賴於序列建模和重建為目標，將臨床標籤視為真實值。<lb/>然而，現實世界中的臨床監督本質上是薄弱的，源於異質的、嘈雜的以及特定機構的標記過程，例如計費代碼、啟發式表型和不完整的註釋。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們提出了WISTERIA，一個弱監督的表徵學習框架，將標籤建模為潛在臨床狀態的隨機觀察。<lb/>WISTERIA不僅僅是針對單一監督信號進行優化，而是構建多個弱監督運算子，並通過強制其引發的標籤分佈之間的一致性來學習表徵。<lb/>這一多視角的表述引入了一種隱式去噪機制，使模型能夠通過調和嘈雜標籤者之間的分歧來恢復臨床上有意義的結構。<lb/>我們進一步在標籤空間中納入本體感知的正則化，以在監督信號上施加語義結構。<lb/>實證結果顯示，WISTERIA在標準EHR基準上提高了預測性能，對標籤噪聲展現出強大的魯棒性，並且相比於基於序列的預訓練目標，展現出更優越的跨機構泛化能力。<lb/>這些結果表明，明確建模監督過程而不是將標籤視為固定目標，為從EHR數據中學習穩健且臨床上有意義的表徵提供了更合適的歸納偏差。
2605.09765.author: Ruan Dong et.al.
2605.09765.authors: Ruan Dong, Yuanyun Zhang, Shi Li
